content:
# Luke Chapter 22 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), PDF pages 115-117 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/LUK_022_page_001.jpg, LUK_022_page_002.jpg, LUK_022_page_003.jpg

---

**1** Now the feast of Unleavened Bread, which is called the Passover, was drawing near.

**2** And the chief priests and the scribes were seeking how they might destroy him, for they feared the people.

**3** And Satan entered into Judas, the one called Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve.

**4** And having gone away, he conferred with the chief priests and officers how he might deliver him up to them.

**5** And they rejoiced, and agreed to give him money.

**6** And he consented, and was seeking an opportunity to deliver him up to them apart from the crowd.

**7** And the day of Unleavened Bread came, on which it was necessary for the Passover to be sacrificed.

**8** And he sent Peter and John, saying, "Go and prepare for us the Passover, that we may eat."

**9** And they said to him, "Where do you will that we prepare?"

**10** And he said to them, "Behold, when you have entered into the city, a man bearing a pitcher of water shall meet you; follow him into the house into which he enters.

**11** And you shall say to the master of the house, 'The Teacher says to you, Where is the guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?'

**12** And that one shall show you a large upper room, furnished; there make ready."

**13** And having gone, they found it just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.

**14** And when the hour came, he reclined at table, and the apostles with him.

**15** And he said to them, "With desire I have desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer;

**16** for I say to you that I shall not eat it until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God."

**17** And having received a cup, having given thanks, he said, "Take this and divide it among yourselves;

**18** for I say to you that I shall not drink from now on of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes."

**19** And having taken bread, having given thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, "This is my body which is given for you; do this in my remembrance."

**20** And the cup likewise after they had eaten, saying, "This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.

**21** But behold, the hand of the one who delivers me up is with me on the table.

**22** For indeed the Son of Man goes according to what has been determined, but woe to that man by whom he is delivered up."

**23** And they began to inquire among themselves which of them then it might be who was about to do this.

**24** And there arose also a dispute among them as to which of them seemed to be greatest.

**25** And he said to them, "The kings of the nations exercise lordship over them, and those who have authority over them are called benefactors.

**26** But you are not to be so; rather, let the greatest among you become as the youngest, and the one who leads as the one who serves.

**27** For who is greater — the one who reclines at table, or the one who serves? Is it not the one who reclines? But I am in the midst of you as the one who serves.

**28** And you are those who have continued with me in my trials;

**29** and I appoint to you, even as my Father appointed to me, a kingdom,

**30** that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you shall sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

**31** Simon, Simon, behold, Satan has demanded to sift you as wheat;

**32** but I have prayed for you, that your faith may not fail; and you, when you have turned again, strengthen your brothers."

**33** And he said to him, "Lord, with you I am ready to go both to prison and to death."

**34** And he said, "I tell you, Peter, the rooster shall not crow today until you have three times denied that you know me."

**35** And he said to them, "When I sent you without purse and bag and sandals, did you lack anything?" And they said, "Nothing."

**36** And he said to them, "But now, let the one who has a purse take it, and likewise a bag; and the one who has no sword, let him sell his garment and buy one.

**37** For I say to you that this which is written must be fulfilled in me: 'And he was reckoned among the lawless.' For indeed that which concerns me has its fulfillment."

**38** And they said, "Lord, behold, here are two swords." And he said to them, "It is enough."

**39** And having gone out, he went according to his custom to the Mount of Olives; and the disciples also followed him.

**40** And having come to the place, he said to them, "Pray that you enter not into temptation."

**41** And he withdrew from them about a stone's throw, and having knelt down, he was praying,

**42** saying, "Father, if you are willing, take this cup away from me; nevertheless, not my will but yours be done."

**43** And an angel from heaven appeared to him, strengthening him.

**44** And being in agony, he was praying more earnestly; and his sweat became as drops of blood falling down upon the ground.

**45** And having risen from prayer, having come to the disciples, he found them sleeping from sorrow,

**46** and he said to them, "Why do you sleep? Rise and pray, that you enter not into temptation."

**47** While he was still speaking, behold, a crowd, and the one called Judas, one of the twelve, was going before them, and he drew near to Jesus to kiss him.

**48** But Jesus said to him, "Judas, do you deliver up the Son of Man with a kiss?"

**49** And those around him, seeing what was about to happen, said, "Lord, shall we strike with the sword?"

**50** And a certain one of them struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his right ear.

**51** But Jesus answered and said, "Permit even this." And having touched the ear, he healed him.

**52** And Jesus said to those who had come against him — the chief priests and officers of the temple and elders — "As against a robber have you come out with swords and clubs?

**53** When I was with you daily in the temple, you did not stretch out your hands against me. But this is your hour, and the authority of darkness."

**54** And having seized him, they led him away and brought him into the house of the high priest. And Peter was following at a distance.

**55** And when they had kindled a fire in the midst of the courtyard and had sat down together, Peter was sitting in the midst of them.

**56** And a certain maidservant, seeing him sitting by the light, and having gazed at him, said, "This one also was with him."

**57** But he denied it, saying, "Woman, I do not know him."

**58** And after a little while, another saw him and said, "You also are one of them." But Peter said, "Man, I am not."

**59** And after about one hour had passed, a certain other man was insisting strongly, saying, "In truth this one also was with him, for he is also a Galilean."

**60** But Peter said, "Man, I do not know what you are saying." And immediately, while he was still speaking, the rooster crowed.

**61** And the Lord, having turned, looked upon Peter. And Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said to him, "Before the rooster crows today, you shall deny me three times."

**62** And having gone outside, he wept bitterly.

**63** And the men who were holding him were mocking him, beating him;

**64** and having blindfolded him, they were asking him, saying, "Prophesy! Who is the one who struck you?"

**65** And many other things they were saying against him, blaspheming.

**66** And when it became day, the assembly of the elders of the people was gathered together, both chief priests and scribes, and they led him away into their council, saying,

**67** "If you are the Christ, tell us." And he said to them, "If I tell you, you will by no means believe;

**68** and if I ask you, you will by no means answer.

**69** But from now on the Son of Man shall be seated at the right hand of the power of God."

**70** And they all said, "You then are the Son of God?" And he said to them, "You say that I am."

**71** And they said, "What further need have we of testimony? For we ourselves have heard from his own mouth."

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Manuscript and Textual Observations

**Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph / 01), fourth century. The text of Luke 22 spans three folio pages (PDF pp. 115-117). Column markers at the bottom of each page confirm the verse ranges: page 115 covers approximately Lk 21:24 to 22:11; page 116 covers approximately 22:12 to 22:44; page 117 covers approximately 22:45 to 23:7.

**Verses 43-44 (The Bloody Sweat):** This is one of the most significant textual-critical questions in the New Testament. Codex Sinaiticus **includes** these verses in the original hand — the angel strengthening Jesus and the sweat like drops of blood are present in the manuscript as read on page 116 (column 4). However, it should be noted that these verses are **absent** from some important witnesses (notably Papyrus 75 and Codex Vaticanus, B/03), and some scholars regard them as a later interpolation. The corrector of Sinaiticus also appears to have marked these verses. The LOGOS Bible retains them here because they are present in the base manuscript being translated. The reader should be aware that their originality is disputed.

### Key Translation Decisions

**v.15 — "With desire I have desired" (epithumia epethumesa):** The Hebraic cognate construction is preserved ("with desire I have desired") rather than flattening to "I have earnestly desired," as it conveys the intensity and Semitic register of Jesus' speech.

**v.19 — "remembrance" (anamnesis):** Rendered "remembrance" consistently, preserving the liturgical weight of the term. Not reduced to "memory."

**v.20 — "new covenant" (kaine diatheke):** Sinaiticus reads "the new covenant in my blood." The term "covenant" is maintained rather than "testament," following the theological force of diatheke in its covenantal context.

**v.25 — "benefactors" (euergetai):** Retained as "benefactors" — this was a title of honor for Hellenistic rulers, and the irony of Jesus' statement depends on preserving the specific word.

**v.31 — "sift you as wheat":** The pronoun "you" in "sift you" is plural (humas) in the Greek — Satan demanded all the disciples. But "I have prayed for you" in v.32 is singular (sou) — Jesus prayed specifically for Peter. English obscures this distinction; the note preserves it.

**v.37 — "reckoned among the lawless" (meta anomōn elogisthē):** Quotation from Isaiah 53:12 (LXX). "Lawless" is preferred over "transgressors" to maintain the alpha-privative force of anomos (without law).

**v.42 — "take this cup away from me":** Sinaiticus reads parenengke, the aorist imperative of parapherō. "Take away" preserves the directness of the petition.

**v.44 — "drops of blood" (thromboi haimatos):** The word thromboi means "clots" or "thick drops." Rendered "drops of blood" for readability while preserving the vivid physicality of the description.

**v.51 — "Permit even this" (eate heōs toutou):** A notoriously difficult phrase. It could mean "Allow this much" (i.e., stop, let it go no further) or "Let them be — even this" (i.e., allow even this arrest). The translation "Permit even this" maintains the ambiguity present in the Greek.

**v.69 — "the power of God":** Sinaiticus reads tēs dunameōs tou theou — "the power of God." This circumlocution for the divine name echoes Daniel 7:13 and the Synoptic parallels.

**v.70 — "You say that I am" (humeis legete hoti egō eimi):** This enigmatic reply is preserved in its exact form. It is both an affirmation and a deflection — Jesus places the declaration in their mouths while not refusing it.

### Consistent Term Renderings in Luke 22
| Greek Term | LOGOS Rendering |
|---|---|
| paradidōmi | deliver up |
| to pascha | the Passover |
| archiereis | chief priests |
| grammateis | scribes |
| peirasmos | temptation |
| ho huios tou anthrōpou | the Son of Man |
| diathēkē | covenant |
| basileia tou theou | kingdom of God |
| alektōr | rooster |

— LOGOS
